facts.text: Rochester earned nicknames like 'the Flour City' and 'the Flower City' while also becoming the 'World's Image Center' for film and optics.
Shortly after its founding, Rochester attracted a wave of English‑Puritan immigrants from New England seeking new agricultural land.
In 1847 abolitionist Frederick Douglass launched The North Star newspaper right in Rochester, turning the city into a hub for anti‑slavery voices.
Entrepreneur George Eastman founded Kodak in 1892 and later donated millions to build the George Eastman Museum and other cultural institutions.
Rochester is the birthplace of Xerox, founded in 1906 as the Haloid Company before becoming a global copier giant.
During World War II, Rochester factories produced optical equipment and radio proximity fuses worth about $1.2 billion in military orders.
